behavior: transport=bastion adds a ProxyCommand (SSOT-derived) to every mesh-only node and none to the public-IP VPS
evidence: 2026-07-10: pytest -k bastion → 2 passed. Smoke (real generate --transport bastion): ace2 & aws1 carry `ProxyCommand=ssh -i ~/.ssh/id_ed25519 -W %h:%p -q ... deployer@<vps.public_ip>`; kubelab-vps has no per-host args (inherits global, jump-free).

behavior: Default / transport=mesh reproduces the current inventory unchanged (regression guard)
evidence: 2026-07-10: pytest -k mesh → 3 passed (no per-host ssh args in mesh; global args unchanged; mesh is the default). Full non-e2e suite: 375 passed, 0 failed.

behavior: The bastion target derives from networking.* SSOT — no public IP hardcoded in generator source
evidence: 2026-07-10: grep finds 0 dotted-quad IPs in generator_ansible.py (exit 1). Bastion host/user/key derived from networking.vps.public_ip / ssh_users.cloud / networking.ssh_key.

behavior: CLI exposes --transport and the Makefile provision target threads TRANSPORT= into generate
verification: poetry run toolkit infra ansible generate --help | grep -q -- '--transport' && grep -q 'TRANSPORT' Makefile
evidence: 2026-07-10: `--transport` present in `ansible generate --help` (validated to mesh|bastion); Makefile provision regenerates via `--transport $(TRANSPORT)` then restores mesh.

behavior: The new AnsibleGenerator unit suite passes (backfills previously-absent coverage)
evidence: 2026-07-10: tests/test_generator_ansible.py → 5 passed. ruff clean, mypy clean on generator_ansible.py.

behavior: End-to-end: make provision NODE=x TRANSPORT=bastion reaches a mesh-only node from a non-mesh Linux controller through the bastion (RUNTIME, Linux-gated)
verification: make provision NODE=ace2 ENV=staging TRANSPORT=bastion --tags dev_node
